name: Appenzell Innerrhoden (Switzerland)
description: A square white field charged with a black bear rampant, with red claws, tongue and genitals.
history: The Appenzell bear derives from the banner of the Abbot of St. Gallen, the field changed to white for the independent territory. Innerrhoden has borne the bear since Appenzell split along confessional lines in 1597.

features.role: bear rampant, armed and langued red
features.symbolism: the bear of the Abbey of Saint Gall, retained by Appenzell
